steps.label: Objectives
steps.bodyHtml:
i. Use the alternating series test to test an alternating series for convergence

ii. Estimate the sum of an alternating series

ii. Explain the meaning of absolute convergence and conditional convergence

steps.label: Alternating Series
steps.bodyHtml:
Any series whose terms alternate between positive and negative values is called an alternating series. An alternating series can be written in the form \[\displaystyle\sum_{n=1}^{\infty}(-1)^{n+1}b_n=b_1-b_2+b_3-b_4+\cdots\]or
  \[\displaystyle\sum_{n=1}^{\infty}(-1)^nb_n=-b_1+b_2-b_3+\cdots\]where \(b_n>0\) for all positive integers n.

steps.label: Alternating Series Test
steps.bodyHtml:
An alternating series converges if

\(0 < b_{n+1}\leq b_n\) and

ii. \(\displaystyle\lim_{n\to\infty}b_n=0\)

steps.label: Exercises
steps.bodyHtml:
Find if the next series converges or diverges

a) \(\displaystyle\sum_{n=1}^{\infty}(-1)^{n+1}\frac{1}{n^2}\)

b) \(\displaystyle\sum_{n=1}^{\infty}(-1)^{n+1}\frac{n}{n+1}\)

steps.label: Remainders in the Alternating Series
steps.bodyHtml: Conside an alternating series that satisfies the hypothesis of the alternating series test. Meaning that converges. Let S denote the sum of the series and \(S_N\) denotes the N-th partial sum. For any integer \(N\geq1\), the remainder \(R_N=S-S_N\) satisfies that \[|R_N|\leq b_{n+1}\]
